description: Classify state and non-state actors along cooperation/competition and globalization/nationalism axes to frame geopolitical analysis of investment impacts

actor_types.state_actors: Sovereign governments setting foreign, trade, and security policy
actor_types.non_state_actors: Multinationals, NGOs, supranationals, armed groups, individuals with global influence

cooperation_vs_competition.cooperation: Countries align on rules, trade, standards — lowers transaction costs
cooperation_vs_competition.competition: Countries pursue conflicting objectives — raises risk premia

globalization_vs_nationalism.globalization: Open flows of goods, capital, labour, data across borders
globalization_vs_nationalism.nationalism: Priority on domestic interests, protectionism, migration limits

four_archetypes.globalization_cooperation: Multilateralism — WTO, EU single market style
four_archetypes.globalization_competition: Open rivalry — race for standards, economic statecraft
four_archetypes.nationalism_cooperation: Regional blocs with inward focus — e.g., selective alliances
four_archetypes.nationalism_competition: Autarky / confrontation — trade wars, sanctions, deglobalization

tools_of_geopolitics.economic: Sanctions, tariffs, export controls, capital controls
tools_of_geopolitics.financial: SWIFT access, reserve currency, IMF / World Bank

validation.valid_axes: both axes specified from allowed set

classify_actor.description: Place actor in the four-quadrant framework

errors.message: axis values must come from the allowed set

anti_patterns.rule: Do not treat geopolitical archetypes as static
anti_patterns.why: Countries move across quadrants over decades; regimes change and alliances shift

anti_patterns.rule: Do not reduce geopolitics to military conflict alone
anti_patterns.why: Economic and financial statecraft (sanctions, export controls) are dominant modern tools
Multinationals, supranationals, and armed groups often drive outcomes states cannot control

anti_patterns.rule: Do not assume cooperation implies friendship
anti_patterns.why: Transactional cooperation can coexist with strategic rivalry; frame by interests, not sentiment

goals.description: Classify state and non-state actors along cooperation/competition and globalization/nationalism axes to frame geopolitical analysis of investment impacts

success_metrics.measurement: All decisions have complete audit trails

constraints.description: All operations must be auditable and traceable

tradeoffs.reason: trading operations require precise execution and full audit trails

invariants: error messages never expose internal system details
